name: The Pale Shepherd Guide of Transitions
description:
The Pale Shepherd Guide of Transitions

*{Lore The Pale Shepherd walks the seam between all things -- life and death, joy and sorrow, memory and forgetting, the known and the unmarked. Neither wholly kind nor cruel, the Shepherd guides those who must pass through the hardest doors. Cloaked, with shadow-sheep that bear the shed wool of old possibilities, the Shepherd offers safe conduct through liminal space for a price paid in memory, feeling, or fate. Those who walk this road learn the hidden routes between states of being and can lead others through their hardest crossings -- but each passage leaves them more attuned to endings than beginnings.

The Shepherd has no will, only a path; no demand, only a presence. It does not seek followers, but those who stand at thresholds -- of death, of grief, of irreversible change -- may find the Shepherd already there, waiting to accompany them. Its "demands" are not commands but the natural toll of passage: a memory left behind, a name forgotten, a tear shed for no reason. The Shepherd does not punish refusal; it simply withdraws, and the crossing becomes harder.

Among the Aelaerem, the Shepherd is called the Pale Gardener, who walks the hedgerows at twilight, collecting the souls of those who die far from home. In Aeler, the Breath-Watcher weighs the last exhalation of the dying. In Linn, the Fog-Walker appears on the horizon before a storm. In Viterra, the Silent Notary witnesses deathbed testaments. Wherever mortals face the hardest thresholds, the Pale Shepherd is there -- not to judge, not to command, but to accompany.

quote "The Shepherd goes where feet fail, speaks where voices thin, and points where paths run dim. Each journey loosens the knot that tied you to the life you left behind." quote

*{Domain Focus itemize Liminal Navigation: walking and reading thresholds, dusk-roads, and in-betweens Passage Guidance: steadying hands through grief, change, and transformation Melancholic Wisdom: understanding born of farewells and last looks Memory Shepherding: gathering, carrying, and setting down weighted moments itemize

*{Thiasos and Codex (Runekeeper Options)

A Runekeeper sworn to the Pale Shepherd does not bind a creature of fierce guardianship --- they bind a companion of gentle thresholds. Their Thiasos is a small beast or being that embodies passage and memory: a gray mouse that nests in graveyards and knows every hidden crack, a moth with dusty wings that lands on the hands of the dying, a small lamb with wool the color of ash that appears only at crossroads, a pale fox that leaves no footprints in fresh snow, or a white-breasted raven that calls once when a soul is ready to cross. This creature requires no food, but it must witness a farewell or a threshold crossing each day (a death, a departure, or a significant goodbye). If neglected for a week, it becomes Compromised (it grows silent, refusing to guide; its bearer suffers --1 die to all rolls involving thresholds or transitions until the creature is shown a proper farewell).

The Codex of the Pale Shepherd is never a book of spells alone. It may be a ledger of names written in disappearing ink that reappears only at twilight, a bundle of woolen cords each tied for a soul guided, a series of river stones stacked in a cairn that shifts with each passing, or a smooth slate etched with the outline of a door that never quite closes. Because the Shepherd's truths are written in passage, the Codex requires upkeep: the Runekeeper must spend an hour each week whispering the names of those they have guided, or the Rites stored there become faint (treat as Compromised until re-dedicated at a threshold).

*{Patron's Gift (Imbuement)

Once per scene as an action, a Runekeeper of the Pale Shepherd may touch a held item (weapon, tool, or garment) and whisper a word of crossing. For the rest of the scene, the item grants +1 die to Insight or Medicine rolls when dealing with the dying, the grieving, or those undergoing great change, and the first time each scene the bearer would suffer a Condition from fear or despair, they may ignore it (the Shepherd steadies their hand). After the scene, the user marks +1 Obligation to the Pale Shepherd.
summary: The Pale Shepherd Guide of Transitions *{Lore The Pale Shepherd walks the seam between all things -- life and death, joy and sorrow, memory and forgetting, the known and the unmarked. Neither wholly kind nor cruel, the Shepherd guides those who must pass through the hardest doors.

rites.name: Rite of the Gentle Guide
rites.description: (Basic, 5 XP) Scene; Near/Self; Yes. Tags: , , , Materials: A small stone from a grave or threshold, warmed in the palm; or a thread from a worn garment and a pinch of dawn mist. Effect: Choose one: itemize Gentle Guidance: Ease a hard crossing. One target gains +1 die to rolls involving change, decision, or emotional steadiness this scene. Hidden Path: Find or make a way where none is seen. Gain +2 dice to Navigation or Investigation when seeking unconventional routes. itemize Push It: (Hidden Path only) The way runs through a true between-place; gain the route but mark 1 SB (Spades) as boundary powers take notice. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: Choose one: itemize Gentle Guidance: Ease a hard crossing. One target gains +1 die to rolls involving change, decision, or emotional steadiness this scene. Hidden Path: Find or make a way where none is seen. Gain +2 dice to Navigation or Investigation when seeking unconventional routes. itemize

rites.name: Rite of the Passing Flock
rites.description: (Advanced, 8 XP) Scene; Touch/Zone; Yes. Tags: , , , , Materials: Black wool and a personal token of the one to be guided; or ash of burned letters and wool shaped into little sheep. Effect: Choose one: itemize Shadow Passage: Open a brief passage through shadow-realms. The target bypasses one insurmountable obstacle or reaches a place normally shut. Memory Flock: Call shadow-sheep that carry fragments of weighty moments. Retrieve lost details, soothe grief, or glean counsel from what was. itemize Push It: (Passage) The passage leaves a scent-trail; mark 1 SB (Clubs) as something may follow. (Flock) The memories ride too close to the skin -- participants relive them as their own; mark 1 SB (Hearts). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: Choose one: itemize Shadow Passage: Open a brief passage through shadow-realms. The target bypasses one insurmountable obstacle or reaches a place normally shut. Memory Flock: Call shadow-sheep that carry fragments of weighty moments. Retrieve lost details, soothe grief, or glean counsel from what was. itemize

rites.name: Rite of the Unburdened Farewell
rites.description: (Basic, 6 XP) Instant; Touch; No. Tags: , , , Materials: A personal letter never sent, burned in a copper dish. Effect: Speak a final goodbye to someone who is not present -- a lost lover, a dead parent, a friend you wronged. Clear 1 Fatigue and remove one psychological Condition (e.g., Haunted, Guilty) caused by that relationship. The GM gains 1 SB as the words drift into the threshold. Push It: The farewell also grants +1 die on your next roll related to moving on from that relationship. Mark +1 Obligation. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Instant.
rites.effect: Speak a final goodbye to someone who is not present -- a lost lover, a dead parent, a friend you wronged. Clear 1 Fatigue and remove one psychological Condition (e.g., Haunted, Guilty) caused by that relationship. The GM gains 1 SB as the words drift into the threshold. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Threshold Bond
rites.description: (Master, 13 XP) Extended; Touch; Yes. Tags: , , , Materials: Two keepsakes: one of what is left, one of what is sought. Effect: Bind two states, places, or conditions with a felt thread. Those bound can sense across the threshold and lend one another strength during change. Push It: The bond sets like iron -- permanent and inescapable. Mark +2 Obligation. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +3) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +2 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex + Tier III (Invoke: 2 Boons). Invoke: Extended action. Duration: Permanent.
rites.effect: Bind two states, places, or conditions with a felt thread. Those bound can sense across the threshold and lend one another strength during change. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Final Shepherd
rites.description: (Master, 14 XP) Extended; Zone; Yes. Tags: , , , , Materials: Last words, a much-crossed threshold, and the consent of those who remain. Effect: Take the mantle of perfect guide for a great passage -- death, molt, or life-bend. All present gain +2 dice to navigate the crossing; you shoulder the weight of it. Push It: Guidance is flawless but costly: mark Harm 2 (Weariness) and +2 Obligation. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +4) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +2 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex + Tier III (Invoke: 2 Boons). Invoke: Extended action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: Take the mantle of perfect guide for a great passage -- death, molt, or life-bend. All present gain +2 dice to navigate the crossing; you shoulder the weight of it. \\
